latest.title: Agreement to Acquire Crushing Plant for Andacollo
latest.content:
Today 11:51
RNS Number : 1173M
Galantas Gold Corporation
13 July 2026
GALANTAS GOLD TO ACQUIRE 20,000 TPD CRUSHING PLANT FOR ANDACOLLO GOLD PROJECT, CHILE
§
Major Milestone Significantly Advances Project Execution and Reduces Restart Capital
Toronto, Ontario - July 13, 2026 - Galantas Gold Corporation
("
Galantas
" or the "
Company
") (TSX-V: GAL | AIM: GAL |
OTCQX: GALKF
) announces
that it has entered into a binding agreement to acquire a complete three-stage crushing plant and associated agglomeration plant equipment located in Mexico for installation at its 100%-owned Andacollo Gold Project in Chile, for total consideration of US$4.2 million. Completion remains subject to execution of a definitive asset purchase agreement and satisfaction of customary conditions, including regulatory, corporate, export, import and other third-party approvals.
The crushing plant is designed for a nominal throughput capacity of approximately 20,000 tonnes per day and is reported by the seller to be in good operating condition, subject to Galantas' ongoing technical, legal and title due diligence. The acquisition represents a significant milestone in the Company's restart strategy and forms a key component of the planned development of the Andacollo Gold Project.
To execute the relocation of the plant, Galantas has appointed M3 Engineering & Technology Corporation ("M3"), an internationally recognized engineering firm with extensive experience in the design, construction and delivery of large mining projects throughout the Americas. M3 has commenced the detailed engineering and project execution activities, including plant documentation, disassembly planning, logistics, transportation engineering, and preparation for reassembly and commissioning in Chile.
With the crushing facility now secured, the Company expects gold production at Andacollo Gold to commence in Q1 2027.
Mario Stifano, CEO of Galantas, commented: "This acquisition is another major execution milestone for Galantas and materially advances the development of the Andacollo Gold Project. Securing a high-quality, proven crushing plant significantly reduces project execution risk, shortens the development schedule and represents substantial capital savings compared with constructing a new crushing facility. Together with our engineering partners at M3, we have now transitioned from project planning into active execution."
Robert Sedgemore, SVP of Operations of Galantas, continued: "Our project execution team is now fully mobilized at Andacollo Gold. Engineering has commenced, procurement activities are accelerating, site infrastructure work is progressing, and we continue to advance the permitting,
construction and operational readiness programs required to bring Andacollo Gold back into production."
Project Execution Underway
Galantas has assembled an experienced technical, engineering and operations team to lead execution of the restart program. Current activities include:
§
Detailed engineering for crushing plant relocation and installation
§
Site preparation and infrastructure upgrades
§
Procurement and contracting for key project work packages
§
Operational readiness planning
§
Mine planning and development activities
§
Recruitment and expansion of the Chilean operating team
§
Advancement of environmental, permitting and regulatory activities
Figure 1. Photos of the three-stage crushing plant being acquired by Galantas
About Galantas Gold Corporation
Galantas Gold Corporation is a publicly traded gold and copper company focused on the acquisition, development, and advancement of gold and copper assets in stable mining jurisdictions. The Company is currently advancing the development of the Indiana Proj
ect and the Andacollo Gold Project in Chile. Galantas' strategy is to build long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, technically rigorous project evaluation, and responsible development of high-quality mineral assets.
Neither TSXV nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXV) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release. The Company is admitted to trading on AIM and accordingly, further disclosure on the matter can be found on the Company's profile on the London Stock Exchange website.
The information contained within this announcement is deemed to constitute inside information as stipulated under the retained EU law version of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 (the "UK MAR") which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018. The information is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of the UK MAR. Upon the publication of this announcement, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.

latest.content:

RNS Number : 9758L
Galantas Gold Corporation
13 July 2026
GALANTAS GOLD ANNOUNCES THE EXERCISE OF WARRANTS
Toronto, Ontario - July 13, 2026 - Galantas Gold Corporation
(TSX-V: GAL | AIM: GAL) ("
Galantas
" or the "
Company
") announces on
9 July 2026, warrants over 150,000 ordinary shares in the Company were exercised at an exercise price of
C$0.12
per ordinary share.
Application will be made to the London Stock Exchange for the 150,000 new ordinary shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). It is expected that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the new ordinary shares on AIM will commence on or around 16 July 2026. The new ordinary shares will rank
pari passu
with the existing ordinary shares in the Company.
Following Admission, the Company's issued share capital will consist of 830,901,823 ordinary shares.
About Galantas Gold Corporation
Galantas Gold Corporation is a publicly traded gold and copper company focused on the acquisition, development, and advancement of gold and copper assets in stable mining jurisdictions. The Company is currently advancing the development of the Indiana Proj

latest.title: Resignation of Chair
latest.content:
8 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4134L
Galantas Gold Corporation
08 July 2026
GALANTAS ANNOUNCES RESIGNATION OF CHAIR OF THE BOARD
Toronto, Ontario - July 8, 2026 - Galantas Gold Corporation
(TSX-V: GAL | AIM: GAL) ("
Galantas
" or the "
Company
") announces that Ms. Róisín Magee has resigned as a member of the Board of Directors and Chair of the Company, effective immediately. Róisín has decided to move to a Senior Role in private equity. The Board of Directors has nominated Mr. David Cather, a Director of the Company, as Interim Chair. Following the resignation, the Board of Directors of the Company consists of five directors.
Mario Stifano, President and CEO of Galantas, commented: "On behalf of the entire Board, I want to thank Róisín for her valued contributions to Galantas and wish her well in her future endeavours."
About Galantas Gold Corporation
Galantas Gold Corporation is a publicly traded gold and copper company focused on the acquisition, development, and advancement of gold and copper assets in stable mining jurisdictions. The Company is currently advancing the development of the Indiana Proj
ect and the Andacollo Gold Project in Chile. Galantas' strategy is to build long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, technically rigorous project evaluation, and responsible development of high-quality mineral assets.

company_30s.what_they_do: GAL.L is a junior gold mining company that owns and operates the Andacollo gold‑mining project in Chile. The company is focused on developing the site’s gold resources through mining, crushing and processing operations.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Gold mining remains a key driver of the commodities market, and Chile is one of the world’s top gold‑producing countries. GAL.L’s Andacollo project sits on a high‑grade deposit that could contribute to global supply if fully developed, giving the company a strategic position in a growing market.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently closed a private placement and is in the process of building its crushing plant. It is still in the early stages of production and is burning cash to get the operation up and running. The share price is trading very low, reflecting a steep decline from its previous peak.

rns_narrative: The company has been actively investing in its Andacollo mine. In mid‑July it announced the acquisition of a crushing plant to accelerate production, and earlier in June it completed the acquisition of the Andacollo project itself. These moves signal a pivot from a pre‑production phase to a near‑production stage.

Alongside capital‑raising activity, GAL.L has been restructuring its executive team—resigning a chair, appointing a new CFO, and granting stock awards to key personnel—indicating a push to strengthen governance and align incentives with shareholders.
Financial filings show the company’s quarterly results and an annual information form, suggesting it is meeting regulatory disclosure requirements while working to build a viable operating model.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism: investors are largely silent but a handful of bullish voices highlight the crushing‑plant deal and upcoming assay results.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the new crushing plant and the potential for the first gold production run. Some analysts note that the plant acquisition could cut ramp‑up time by several months.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the company’s heavy cash burn, the fact that it is still a pre‑production operation, and the risk that the Andacollo project may not meet expected grades or timelines.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has been quiet for most of the year; the recent surge in bullish posts around the crushing‑plant acquisition marks a shift from silence to cautious optimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat—no recent search spikes—so the market is unaware of the latest developments, creating a perfect surprise window.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the crushing‑plant installation and the first production run at Andacollo. A successful start‑up would validate the company’s operational plan and trigger a positive market reaction.
Publication of the first assay results from the new plant. If the grades meet or exceed expectations, it will strengthen the case for future production and potentially raise the share price.
Any announcement of additional funding or a partnership that reduces the company’s debt load and extends its runway.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that coincides with a breakout above the recent 3‑month high, a clear confirmation of institutional interest. Also monitor the company’s earnings releases for any sign of cash burn easing or a shift in capital structure.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a post‑crash recovery: the recent capitulation is complete, the price has stabilized at a very low level, and the APEX score (if >8) would suggest a tight range ready to spring. A mean‑reversion bounce is likely once the first operational milestone is achieved.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash has left the stock at a bargain relative to the underlying asset. If the crushing plant goes live and assay results are positive, the share price could rebound to at least half of its pre‑crash level, delivering roughly a 100% upside. Historically, red and purple signals have produced double‑digit returns; even a modest rebound would represent a significant gain.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still faces operational risks: the mine could under‑grade, the crushing plant might face delays, and cash burn could force a costly capital raise or a partial shutdown. If the project stalls, the share price could fall to the bottom of the current range or worse if the company cannot meet regulatory or financial obligations.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Upside potential: ~2× (100% gain). Downside: limited to the current price level (≈70% drop from the recent high). The risk/reward ratio is therefore roughly 2:1, with the downside capped by the depth of the crash.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 3‑month high with a volume increase of at least 30% over the average. Allocate 10–15% of discretionary capital; set a stop 10% below the entry level to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the crushing‑plant completion announcement or the first assay release. If the share price moves 5% above the recent high on a spike of 20% volume, consider a small position while keeping a tight stop.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a forced liquidation, delisting, or a material fraud claim, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GAL.L sits at a deep crash level, the crushing‑plant acquisition signals a near‑term catalyst, and the asymmetry of a 2× upside versus a 1× downside makes this a contrarian bullish play.
